issues.display_title: Issue #4088: Align Dropbox upload flow with Google Drive (Dropbox Chooser)
issues.main_description_html:
Context

Dropbox upload currently differs a lot from Google Drive. Google Drive uses the native Google Picker: click button → pick files → auto-upload. Dropbox uses a custom-built folder browser that requires connecting a Dropbox account (OAuth), forces files into a specific "GoThru" folder, and only lets users select whole folders (not individual files).

This is inconsistent UX and extra maintenance.

Goal

Make Dropbox behave like Google Drive by adopting Dropbox Chooser (Dropbox's native file picker, the equivalent of Google Picker).

Scope

  • Replace the existing custom Dropbox flow entirely (folder browser, OAuth account connect, "GoThru" folder requirement).
  • The existing Dropbox tab: single button → opens native Dropbox Chooser → user picks individual files (multi-select) → auto-upload, same as Google Drive.
  • Files upload to the currently selected level, matching Google Drive behavior.

Payload

Dropbox Chooser does not return an access token. Per picked file it returns a temporary direct download link (requested via linkType: "direct", expires ~4h). Uploads use a new source: "dropbox-chooser" (separate backend branch — tracked separately).

FE → POST /panoramas/upload (FormData, one request per file):

field value
token_pano upload ticket from GET /panoramas/token?mid=&level=
source "dropbox-chooser"
file the Chooser link (direct download URL)

Example:

token_pano:  <token>
source:      dropbox-chooser
file:        https://dl.dropboxusercontent.com/.../pano_01.jpg

Acceptance criteria

  • The Dropbox tab shows a single "Open Dropbox" button (no account-connect step).
  • Clicking it opens the native Dropbox Chooser.
  • User can multi-select individual .jpg/.jpeg files.
  • Selected files auto-upload to the active level.
  • Upload progress + result popup behave like Google Drive.
  • Old Dropbox flow (folder browser, OAuth connect, GoThru folder) removed.

Out of scope

  • Changes to Google Drive or local Browse upload.
  • Backend source: "dropbox-chooser" handling (separate project).

Demo by Developer

2026-06-22.16-39-17.mp4

issues.display_title: Issue #1274: Handle Issue Applying Template Creates Duplicate Tour and Leaves Primary Tour Empty (404)
issues.main_description_html:
• Audit Template Generation Logic: Investigate the backend function triggered when a template is applied to identify why it spawns a new instance in the Multi Virtual Tours table/state instead of updating the primary record.
  • Refactor Creation Flow: Update the logic so that the template is injected directly into the primary tour's existing data structure.
  • Remove Redundancy: Ensure the system does not create an empty shell tour that leads to the 404 preview error.
  • UI Alignment: Verify that after applying the template, the primary ""GoThru Virtual Tour"" block displays the correct active link and an accurate visual preview of the template.

Description

Update the token conversion rate for the AI Blur API.

Endpoint: /tours/ai-blur

Change

The conversion rate was originally set to 1 token = 10 AI tokens, and must be changed to 1 token = 20 AI tokens.

Old New
Conversion rate 1 token = 10 AI tokens 1 token = 20 AI tokens

Acceptance Criteria

  1. Calling /tours/ai-blur consumes tokens at the updated rate of 1 token = 20 AI tokens.
  2. Token deduction/calculation reflects the new rate consistently across the AI Blur flow.
  3. No regression in the AI Blur request/response behavior aside from the updated rate.

Demo from Developer

To see implementation for current token calculation, you can see here: GoThruMedia/gothru-dashboard#4087.

issues.display_title: Issue #1391: Add Dropbox-Chooser Upload Source to POST /panoramas/upload
issues.main_description_html:
Context

Frontend is adopting the native Dropbox Chooser to align the Dropbox upload flow with Google Drive (single button → pick files → auto-upload). See the FE task: GoThruMedia/gothru-dashboard#4088.

The Chooser does not return an OAuth access token. Instead it returns a temporary direct download link per file (expires ~4h). This needs a new backend upload branch.

Goal

Add a new upload source dropbox-chooser to POST /panoramas/upload that downloads a panorama directly from the Chooser link — no Dropbox account connection, no Dropbox API, no access token.

Request contract

POST /panoramas/upload (FormData, one request per file):

field value
token_pano upload ticket from GET /panoramas/token?mid=&level=
source dropbox-chooser
file the Chooser direct download URL

Example file: https://dl.dropboxusercontent.com/.../pano_01.jpg

Scope

  • New dropbox-chooser branch that fetches the panorama from the provided URL and runs it through the existing upload/validation pipeline (same as Google Drive / Dropbox: JPEG only, dimension + EXIF checks, resize job).
  • Panorama name derived the same way as the existing dropbox source.
  • Reuses the current persistence + resize flow.

Security requirements

Because file is now a caller-supplied URL, the branch must be hardened against SSRF:

  • Only allow Dropbox download hosts (*.dropboxusercontent.com).
  • Reject URLs resolving to private / loopback / internal addresses.
  • Do not follow redirects.
  • Enforce a maximum download size.

Acceptance criteria

  • source: dropbox-chooser accepted by POST /panoramas/upload.
  • Panorama downloaded from the Chooser link and uploaded to the level from token_pano.
  • Non-Dropbox / internal / oversized / non-JPEG URLs are rejected with a clear error.
  • Behaves consistently with Google Drive / Dropbox uploads (validation, resize, response).

Out of scope

  • Frontend Chooser integration (GoThruMedia/gothru-dashboard#4088).
  • Changes to existing Google Drive, Dropbox, or local upload branches.

Demo from Developer
